auth.signInDescription: Sign in to upload and manage your team's data

footer.disclaimer: Twinspire is a decision support platform. It is not intended to diagnose or treat medical conditions.

hero.valueProp: GPS. Training load. Physio notes. That is what club systems capture. Whoop, Oura, Garmin — what the player measures at home, on rest days, at 6am — that data exists. It just never reaches anyone who can act on it. Twinspire is the layer that connects both streams and builds an individual model from the combined picture.
hero.credibility: Developed at DTU. Presented at ECSS 2026.

problem.label: The Gap
problem.headline: Two Data Streams. Neither Aware of the Other.
problem.p1: Your club captures GPS, gym load, physio notes. Your players privately measure Whoop recovery, Oura sleep, Garmin HRV at 6am on a Sunday before a Monday training. These two streams have never been connected with the proper consent architecture, individual modeling, and GDPR compliance to make them clinically useful. That gap is exactly where the early warning signals live.
problem.block1Body: Training load. GPS output. Session data. Physio observations. All valuable. All incomplete — because they only capture what happens inside club hours.
problem.block2Body: Resting heart rate at home. Recovery scores on rest days. Sleep quality the night before a match. HRV trends across the week. This data exists on the player's wrist. Nobody is reading it in context.
problem.block3Body: One in five footballers re-injures the same muscle. That rate has not moved in a decade — not because the data is absent, but because the individual has never been properly modelled across both streams and over time.
problem.originHeading: The Data Existed. Nothing Read It.
problem.originBody: Twinspire began with a rehabilitation process that followed every expected protocol — monitoring, testing, staged progression, medical clearance. A second injury followed. The signals had been there: in the recovery scores, in the HRV trends, in the load data from training. They existed in separate systems that never spoke to each other, and no model was watching the individual closely enough to notice. Twinspire was built to close that gap.

digitalTwin.sectionLabel: What a Decision Support System Actually Is
digitalTwin.heading: A dashboard displays variables.
digitalTwin.headingAccent: It does not explain what matters, why it matters, or what to do next.
digitalTwin.attribution: Dr. Lorena Martin, USC Marshall — ECSS Lausanne 2026
digitalTwin.intro: At the European College of Sport Science conference in 2026, independent researchers arrived at the same diagnosis of the current market: more data has not produced better decisions, and a single readiness score discards the estimate, its uncertainty, its drivers, and its trajectory. Below is the external checklist they proposed for what a genuine decision support system requires — and where Twinspire stands against it honestly.

individualized.label: Individualized
individualized.body: Every metric is measured against that specific player's own evolving baseline — not a squad average, not a population norm.

dynamicallyUpdated.label: Dynamically Updated
dynamicallyUpdated.body: The model updates continuously with every new data point from both club and personal streams. The longer it runs for a player, the more precisely it understands them.

connectedToDecisions.label: Connected to Decisions
connectedToDecisions.body: Output is framed as a decision — is this player ready to train at full intensity today — not as a data export or a chart to interpret.

explicitAboutUncertainty.label: Explicit About Uncertainty
explicitAboutUncertainty.body: The model surfaces its key drivers explicitly. Coaching and medical staff see which signals are driving any deviation, not just that a deviation exists. Not a black box score.

validatedAgainstOutcomes.label: Validated Against Outcomes
validatedAgainstOutcomes.body: This is the criterion the current validation study is designed to close. We are honest about the fact that it is not yet fully met, and that closing it is the specific purpose of the founding partner cohort.

digitalTwin.closingNote: We use this checklist internally. We think you should hold us to it.

science.label: How It Works
science.heading: Club data. Personal data.
science.intro: Twinspire sits between your existing club platform and the player's personal wearables. Club data comes in via API — no platform switch required. Player data comes in via the Twinspire app, which the athlete consents to once. Both streams feed the individual baseline model. The output — readiness scores, deviation alerts, trend data — goes back into your existing setup.

modeling.title: Works With Your Current Setup
modeling.body: API integration with your existing platform — Catapult, Statsports, Bricks, or any provider with an open API. No ripping out infrastructure. No migration. The model sits underneath, and outputs appear in your current interface.
modeling.bullet3: Outputs feed back into your current UI

signals.title: Connects the Private Data Stream
signals.body: Whoop, Oura, Garmin, Polar — the wearables your players already use off-site. Twinspire is the only platform with the consent architecture and GDPR compliance to legitimately integrate personal wearable data into club performance analytics.
signals.bullet2: Player consents once — data stays theirs

validation.title: Individual Baseline Modeling
validation.body: The combined data stream builds a continuously updated model of what normal looks like for this specific player. Not for footballers in general — for him. Deviations from that personal baseline are surfaced early, before they become injuries.
validation.bullet3: Key drivers surfaced, not just a score

science.researchTitle: The model answers three questions every day:

questions.q1: Is this player's current state within their personal normal range — combining what the club measured and what they privately measured?
questions.q2: Which signals are driving any deviation, and is the pattern coming from club-side data, personal data, or both?
questions.q3: Is this player ready to train and play at full intensity — according to their individual model, not a population benchmark?

trust.body: GDPR-compliant from the ground up. EU servers. Player data is never sold and never shared. The athletic passport belongs to the athlete — it moves with them when they transfer. The squad analytics belong to the club. We host both. We own neither.
trust.bullet1: GDPR compliance and Data Processing Agreements
trust.disclaimer: Twinspire is a decision support platform. It is not intended to diagnose or treat medical conditions.

product.p1: When your doctor runs blood tests, they do not just compare you to the population average. They track your values over time because your normal might be different from the reference range. Twinspire does exactly that for every metric in your squad — continuously, and across both the data the club controls and the data the player privately generates.

card1.title: Not a squad benchmark
card1.body: A shift that looks unremarkable when averaged across a squad can be a significant warning for a particular player. Twinspire builds each individual's reference behavior separately and measures everything against it.

card2.title: Trained staff leave. The model does not.
card2.body: A good physio intuitively knows their long-term players. When staff turn over, that knowledge leaves. Twinspire makes it objective, permanent, and transferable.

card3.title: The Athletic Passport
card3.body: Every session, load, injury, and rehab milestone — structured into a portable record that belongs to the athlete. When they transfer, their full history transfers. For clubs, that means inheriting objective data on every new signing from day one.

product.headline2: No Extra App for the Player
product.p2: The most common objection we hear from clubs: we cannot ask our players to use another app. If your current platform already has a player-facing app, Twinspire can operate entirely in the background. The player's wearables sync to Twinspire automatically. The output appears in your existing interface. Twinspire is invisible to the player's daily workflow — and the data it generates is not.
product.mockupAlt: Twinspire readiness dashboard showing individual player metrics

passport.heading: Every time a player transfers,
passport.headingAccent: their physiological history resets to zero.
passport.intro: A new club inherits a name, a transfer fee, and a medical record. What they do not inherit is 18 months of individual baseline data — what load that specific body responds well to, what recovery looks like on a good week, where the personal thresholds actually sit. They start from scratch. Every time.
passport.howItWorks: The Athletic Passport changes that. Every session, load, injury, and rehabilitation milestone is structured into a portable, consent-based record that belongs to the athlete — not the club, not the platform. When they sign for a new club, their baseline arrives with them. The new club does not spend months re-establishing what normal looks like. They already know.
passport.forClubs: For clubs, this means inheriting objective physiological data on every new signing from day one. For players, it means their career-long performance history is portable and owned by them — not left behind at a previous club's platform when they move on.
passport.differentiator: No platform at ECSS 2026 demonstrated cross-club physiological continuity built on a proper consent and GDPR architecture. This is what distinguishes Twinspire from single-club deployment tools: the data does not belong to the club. It belongs to the athlete, and it follows them.

passportItems.item1: Individual baseline, continuously updated across every club
passportItems.item2: Full load, injury, and rehabilitation history
passportItems.item3: Portable consent — the athlete controls who sees what
passportItems.item4: New club inherits objective data from day one of signing

builtFor.heading: Who Uses
builtFor.subtitle: One platform, three roles. Each sees what matters to them — and nothing slows anyone down.

footballers.title: Footballers
footballers.description: Connect your Whoop, Oura, or Garmin once. Your data generates your individual baseline. It belongs to you — not the club, not the platform. When you move clubs, it moves with you. Twinspire is the only system that gives you a portable record of your own physiology across your entire career.

coaches.title: Coaches & Performance Staff
coaches.description: Squad-wide readiness before every session. Individual deviation alerts when a player's pattern shifts outside their personal norms. You already have GPS and gym data — Twinspire adds the layer your current platform is missing: the player's private signal, modelled against their individual baseline.

physio.title: Physiotherapists
physio.description: The early warning you have been relying on intuition for becomes objective and automatic. Twinspire surfaces deviation patterns across both club-controlled and player-controlled data — before they become conversations, and well before they become injuries.

cta.title: We Are Selecting Founding Partner Clubs
cta.subtitle: Twinspire is at validation stage — deploying with a small number of clubs across the Nordics to close the outcomes validation gap. Founding partner clubs shape the product directly, contribute to the validation dataset, and secure preferential terms ahead of commercial launch. We are being selective about who we work with at this stage.
cta.note: No commitment. A short call to understand your current setup and whether this is the right fit.

partners.heading: Platform and Sensor Partners.
partners.body: Twinspire is built to integrate, not compete. If you run a club performance platform, wearable sensors, or sports science tools — and your customers are asking questions that squad-level data cannot answer at the individual level — let us talk.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: The questions clubs, athletes, and partners ask us most often.

whatIsTwinspire.question: What is Twinspire?
whatIsTwinspire.answer: Twinspire is an individual athlete intelligence platform. It connects the data your club platform already captures with the personal wearable data your players generate off-site — and builds a continuously updated individual baseline model for each athlete. The output feeds back into your existing setup as readiness scores, deviation alerts, and trend data. It is a decision support tool for coaching and medical staff, not a dashboard.

whatStageIsThisAt.question: What stage is Twinspire at?
whatStageIsThisAt.answer: Twinspire is at validation stage. The platform is deployed with a small founding partner cohort of clubs across the Nordics. The specific purpose of this cohort is to close the outcomes validation gap — the criterion on the external checklist we are honest about not yet fully meeting. We are not claiming live commercial traction we do not have. We are being precise about where we are and what the validation study is designed to close.

doWeNeedToSwitchPlatforms.question: Do we need to switch platforms to use Twinspire?
doWeNeedToSwitchPlatforms.answer: No. Twinspire integrates with your current setup via API. Whether you use Catapult, Statsports, Bricks, or a custom internal platform — Twinspire sits underneath it, processing the combined data and returning outputs that appear inside your existing interface. No migration. No disruption to current workflows.

whatAboutThePlayerApp.question: We already give our players an app. We cannot ask them to use two.
whatAboutThePlayerApp.answer: We hear this from almost every club. If you have an existing player-facing app, Twinspire can operate entirely in the background. The player's wearables sync to Twinspire automatically through a one-time consent setup. The readiness and deviation data generated appears in your staff dashboard — not in a separate player-facing interface. Twinspire is invisible to the player's daily workflow.

whatWearablesDoYouSupport.question: Which wearables does Twinspire integrate with?
whatWearablesDoYouSupport.answer: Whoop, Oura, Garmin, and Polar are currently supported, with further integrations in development. These are the devices your players most commonly use privately — the ones capturing recovery, sleep, and HRV data off-site that your club platform currently has no access to. If you have a specific device in mind, reach out and we will confirm where it sits on the roadmap.

isThisReallyDifferentFromOurPlatform.question: How is this different from what our current platform does?
isThisReallyDifferentFromOurPlatform.answer: Your current platform captures what happens inside club hours — GPS output, gym load, physio notes. It has no access to what the player privately measures: Whoop recovery on a rest day, Oura sleep the night before a match, HRV at 6am on a Sunday. Twinspire is the only platform built to connect both streams under a proper GDPR and consent framework, and to model the individual against their own personal baseline rather than a squad or population average.

whatIsAthleticPassport.question: What is the Athletic Passport and why does it matter for transfers?
whatIsAthleticPassport.answer: Every time a player transfers, their physiological history resets to zero. A new club inherits a name, a transfer fee, and a medical record — but not 18 months of individual baseline data. The Athletic Passport changes that. It is a structured, portable record of everything a player's body has generated — sessions, loads, injuries, rehab milestones, and the individual baseline built from both club and personal data. It belongs to the athlete, not the club. When they sign for a new club, their baseline arrives with them. The new club does not spend months re-establishing what normal looks like. They already know.

canWePartnerWithTwinspire.question: We are a platform provider. Can we partner with Twinspire?
canWePartnerWithTwinspire.answer: Yes. Twinspire is built to integrate, not compete. If you run a club performance platform and your customers are asking questions that squad-level data cannot answer at the individual level — or if you want to add private wearable integration with proper consent management — we should talk. The same applies to wearable manufacturers and sensor providers. Reach out at info@twinspire.ai.

howDoesExplainabilityWork.question: Is the model a black box? Can staff understand why it flags something?
howDoesExplainabilityWork.answer: No. The model surfaces its key drivers explicitly at the individual level. When a deviation is flagged, coaching and medical staff see which specific signals are driving it — whether it is coming from club-side load data, personal HRV, recovery scores, or a combination — not just that a deviation exists. The output is framed as a decision with visible reasoning, not a score to interpret. This was a design requirement from the start, not a feature added later.

whoOwnsData.question: Who owns the player data?
whoOwnsData.answer: The player owns their Athletic Passport — the longitudinal record of their individual baseline, built from both club and personal data. The club owns its squad analytics. Twinspire hosts both under a formal Data Processing Agreement. Player data is never sold, never shared with third parties, and is fully portable — when a player transfers, their baseline history transfers with them.

isTwinspireGdprCompliant.question: Is Twinspire GDPR compliant?
isTwinspireGdprCompliant.answer: Yes, and it is built to a higher standard than most sports platforms. Twinspire is designed with medical device regulation principles, full GDPR compliance, EU-based hosting, and formal Data Processing Agreements for all club integrations. This is a structural differentiator — most performance platforms were not built with this level of compliance architecture, which increasingly matters as player associations and club legal teams scrutinise health data handling.

whatIsValidationStudy.question: What is the validation study and why does it matter?
whatIsValidationStudy.answer: At ECSS 2026, independent researchers proposed a checklist for what a genuine decision support system requires. One criterion — validated against outcomes — is the specific one Twinspire's current validation study is designed to close. We are deploying with a founding partner cohort to build the longitudinal dataset needed to demonstrate that the individual baseline model predicts deviation events that matter clinically, not just that it detects statistical anomalies. We think it is more credible to be honest about this gap and show how we are closing it than to claim it is already closed.

whereIsTwinspireBased.question: Where is Twinspire based and who built it?
whereIsTwinspireBased.answer: Twinspire was founded at the Technical University of Denmark (DTU) in Copenhagen. The platform was started by Pouya Tobias Strand Nikoui — a former professional footballer who suffered a reinjury after full clinical clearance, and returned to DTU to build what the sport was missing. The team combines expertise in AI, individual baseline modelling, platform architecture, and data security. Backed by DTU Skylab and DIF Innovation Lab.

howToGetStarted.question: How do we get started?
howToGetStarted.answer: Apply to join the founding partner cohort via the form on this page. There is no commitment to apply — just a short call to understand your current setup and whether this is the right fit for both sides. Contact us directly at info@twinspire.ai.

dataEthics.heading: The Compliance Architecture
dataEthics.subtitle: GDPR, medical device standards, and data ownership — designed in from the start, not retrofitted.
dataEthics.description: As player unions and club legal teams increasingly scrutinise how health data is collected and used, the compliance architecture your platform is built on matters. Twinspire was designed from the ground up with the standards that will eventually be required across the industry.
dataEthics.card1: The Athletic Passport belongs to the athlete — not the club, not the platform. The squad analytics belong to the club. Twinspire hosts both under formal Data Processing Agreements. Neither is ever sold or shared with third parties. This is legally watertight in a way most performance platforms are not.
dataEthics.card2: GDPR-aligned from the ground up. EU-based hosting and processing. Built to medical device regulation principles. Formal DPAs available for all club and platform integrations. Granular consent controls — the player decides what they share, and can withdraw consent at any time.
dataEthics.card3: The model is not a black box. Every deviation flag surfaces its key drivers explicitly — which signals, from which data stream, in which direction. Staff see the reasoning, not just the output. This was an architectural requirement, not a feature added after the fact.
dataEthics.card4: Twinspire is a decision support platform. It surfaces signals for qualified staff to act on. It is not intended to diagnose, treat, or replace clinical judgment.
dataEthics.disclaimer: Twinspire is a decision support platform. It is not intended to diagnose, treat, or replace clinical judgment.

team.heading: Built at DTU.
team.headingAccent: By People Who Have Lived the Problem.
team.subtitle: Twinspire was founded at the Technical University of Denmark and is backed by DTU Skylab and DIF Innovation Lab. The team combines professional sport experience, research-grade AI development, platform architecture, and commercial execution.

descriptions.pouya: Pouya played professional football until a reinjury ended his season — after a rehabilitation process that followed every expected protocol. The signals had been there. The tools to read them across both club and personal data streams did not exist. He returned to DTU, turned that experience into a research question, and built what the sport was missing. His MSc in Electrical Engineering, specialising in AI and control systems, became the foundation for Twinspire's individual baseline methodology and the basis for the patent. He leads research translation, strategic direction, and external partnerships.
descriptions.john: John is a Professor at DTU and has spent years leading research into digital twin methodology for physiological systems — work that forms the scientific foundation of Twinspire. His neuromuscular research group is where the project was born, and his lab remains its institutional anchor. Beyond research, John has built and sold technology companies with exits to Facebook, Oculus, and GN Audio, bringing a rare combination of scientific depth and entrepreneurial experience to the team.
descriptions.daryan: Daryan has spent seven years building software at the intersection of architecture, data engineering, and people leadership. At Twinspire he leads the technical implementation that connects club data sources, wearable integrations, and the platform infrastructure that staff and athletes use daily.
descriptions.roxane: Roxane has built and taken AI ventures to market across three continents. She previously co-founded CUJU, an AI sports scouting company developed in partnership with ROGON Sports Management, taking it from zero to exit. Before that she worked with the UN and World Bank on innovation programme development. At Twinspire she leads fundraising, partnerships, and commercial strategy.

joinUs.pageTitleAccent: Help Build the Intelligence Layer Elite Football Is Missing.
joinUs.pageSubtitle: We are a small team solving a hard technical problem where the stakes are real. If you want to build something that matters in sport, we would like to hear from you.
joinUs.heroSubtitle: We believe the best products are built by people who understand the problem from the inside.
